title: Claude TDD Pro v1.9 — Rubric Rule
description: Schema for a single rubric rule per architectural design v1.9 §2.1. Combines E-8 ESLint-parity metadata, G-phase source-folder pointers, S-phase provenance, and C-phase control mappings.

id.description: Stable identifier (e.g., g-ts-001, g-react-007, my-org-001, plugin-id/rule-id).

description.description: One-line description of what the rule enforces.

detector.description: Path (relative to plugin root) to the detector script that implements this rule.

remediation.description: How violations are fixed (free-form prose; references skill or command names where applicable).

source_file.description: G-phase: relative path to the source-folder file this rule lives in.

source_namespace.description: G-phase: top-level source folder (e.g., google, us-government, owasp, react).

fixable.description: ESLint-parity: code fixable, whitespace fixable, or not fixable (null).

has_suggestions.description: ESLint-parity: rule offers suggestion-level fixes that require operator confirmation.

deprecated.description: ESLint-parity: rule is deprecated and should be replaced.

replaced_by.description: ESLint-parity: rule IDs that replace this deprecated rule.

docs_url.description: URL to canonical documentation for this rule.

requires_type_checking.description: ESLint-parity: rule requires TypeScript type information to run (slower).

recommended.description: ESLint-parity: rule is in the source-file's recommended subset.

options_schema.description: JSON Schema for the rule's options array. Operator overrides validate against this.

messages.description: messageId → template map for i18n. Templates may contain {{placeholders}}.

severity.description: Default severity. Profile rules block can override per ESLint pattern.

false_positive_log.description: Path to per-rule false-positive log (operator-triaged via /measure-rubric).

provenance.description: Authoritative source citations. ≥1 required. P0/P1 rules require ≥1 tier-1 published-standard entry per S-6.

source.description: Source ID matching an entry in STANDARDS-URLS.yaml, COMPLIANCE-URLS.yaml, or PR-SOURCES.yaml.

rule_state.description: Default activation state. New rules ship in warn-only per O-7 canary.

legal_review_status.description: Per-framework legal review state. Required for rules with controls citing legal frameworks.

applies_to.description: Either legacy stack/use-case tags (array of strings) OR the ADR-0008 4-axis canonical binding (object). The 4-axis form binds a rule to tools via industry authorities (GitHub Linguist languages, IaC-scanner dialect consensus, PURL package types, Kubernetes GVKs) instead of CTP-invented strings. Mirrors live in vendor/canonical-vocabulary/.

linguist_aliases.description: GitHub Linguist (MIT) language names/aliases (e.g. typescript, python, hcl).

iac_dialects.description: IaC dialects per the Checkov/Trivy/Kubescape consensus (terraform, kubernetes, cloudformation, github_actions, ...).

purl_uses.description: PURL (MIT) package types/coordinates the rule targets (e.g. pkg:npm/react, npm, pypi).

enforced_by.description: ADR-0008 composite-engine tool bindings, walked in order (first matching binding wins per file). Each entry names a FOSS tool runner or the architectural-content bundle.

tool.description: Tool runner id (e.g. semgrep, eslint, checkov) or a detector path.

bundle.description: Bundle id (e.g. architectural-content) auto-attached when applies_to_prose is true.

config.description: Tool config / ruleset reference (a named preset/ruleset, e.g. eslint:recommended, p/typescript, a .rubocop.yml path).

options.description: §28.50 tool-NATIVE options for THIS binding -- the single-layer, tool-specific config (no transformer). Keys + values are the tool's own option vocabulary, drawn from standards/tool-option-surfaces.yaml for that tool (e.g. eslint {complexity:[error,{max:10}]}, ruff {mccabe.max-complexity:10}, rubocop {Max:15}, stylelint {severity:warning}). Overridable per (rule,tool) from the single config surface (ctp.config.yaml). Absent => the tool runs with its defaults.

required.description: When true a missing tool HARD-FAILS (blocks) per the ADR-0008 operator policy; when false/absent the tool is optional and degrades to not_enforced (§28.17).

introduced_in.description: Consumer-compatibility epoch tag (§28.40): the CTP pin/version at which this rule first appeared. 'baseline' = present before the Consumer Compatibility Contract (grandfathered). Consumers gate enforcement floors by epoch — a floor requirement applies only to tickets/content issued at or after the rule's introduced_in epoch — so an additive rule never retroactively reds legacy consumer state.

languages.description: Backwards-compat: v0.3 substrate language tags. Will be deprecated in favor of applies_to.

applies_to_prose.description: When true (default false), this rule also binds architectural Markdown prose (ADR/architecture docs) via rubric/detectors/prose-judge.sh, not only its code-shape detector. The prose-as-code principle (ADR-0007 / §28.24).

applies_to_prose_kinds.description: Which Markdown section kinds (frontmatter kind:, default [architecture, adr]) prose-judge evaluates this rule against. Only meaningful when applies_to_prose is true.
